_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MCh/ DNB or equivalent in (Urology, Pediatric surgery)
clinical_indications: Diagnosis of Undescended Testis (Congenital, acquired, palpable, non-palpable, unilateral, bilateral, cryptorchid/undescended, ectopic, retractile, gliding, acquired types)
Nonpalpable Undescended Testis when diagnosis is backed by clinical manifestations.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management
USG abdomen confirming the need for laparoscopy in locating the testes
Clinical Photograph of the affected part

ppd_preauth.q: Are the clinical notes and USG abdomen confirming the diagnosis of undescended testis and indicative of procedure?

common_queries: Clinical notes and/or USG abdomen do not confirm undescended testis diagnosis or do not indicate the procedure.
USG abdomen confirming the need for laparoscopy in locating the testes is missing.
Clinical photograph of the affected part is missing or of poor quality.
Operative notes are missing details on surgical steps or procedure outcome.
Post-operative clinical photograph (if submitted) is unclear or not useful for review.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Tertiary care and availability of laparoscopic surgery facility
clinical_key_pointers: With Nonpalpable Undescended Testis proceed with Orchiopexy only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical manifestations. Undescended testis
